pdg_node.informal_desc: Create a model instance from physical parameters.

declaration.name: initialize_model
declaration.docstring: Create a model instance from physical parameters.
declaration.conceptual_summary: Constrained Parameter-to-Object Instantiation. Maps a finite tuple of constrained scalar inputs into a typed, immutable parameter object. The operation enforces positivity constraints and packages the validated values into a canonical structure used by downstream pure transformations. No iterative optimization or state mutation occurs; this is a direct constructor mapping from numeric parameters to a stable model representation.. Applications: Initializing a pharmacokinetic simulation entity from absorption and clearance coefficients, Creating a robotics dynamics profile from inertia and drag-like coefficients, Instantiating a network-flow node model from capacity and resistance parameters, Seeding a climate micro-model component from heat-capacity and exchange-surface factors

declaration.name: initialize_model

pdg_node.informal_desc: Compute aerodynamic drag force from velocity.

declaration.name: compute_aerodynamic_force
declaration.docstring: Compute aerodynamic drag force from velocity.
declaration.conceptual_summary: signed_nonlinear_resistive_mapping. Transforms a signed scalar rate into a signed scalar opposing term whose magnitude increases with the rate magnitude (typically second-order in absolute value). The mapping is memoryless and applies an odd-symmetric dissipation law: zero input yields zero output, and reversing input sign reverses output sign while preserving magnitude profile.. Applications: Rotational system modeling: map angular velocity to opposing damping torque in actuator or flywheel simulations, Electromechanical modeling: map current or speed to counteracting loss term for energy dissipation estimation, Biomechanical dynamics: map joint segment velocity to passive resistive force in simplified limb models, Fluid transport networks: map signed flow rate to opposing pressure-drop term in reduced-order pipeline/channel models

pdg_node.informal_desc: Compute rolling resistance force from grade angle.

declaration.name: compute_rolling_force
declaration.docstring: Compute rolling resistance force from grade angle.
declaration.conceptual_summary: orientation_modulated_resistive_scalar. Transforms a single orientation angle into a resistive scalar by applying a trigonometric projection from an inclined frame to its normal component, then scaling to model force units. The mapping is a closed-form arithmetic step that reduces effective resistance as the surface/frame normal component decreases with increasing inclination magnitude.. Applications: Mobile robotics: estimating contact-loss-adjusted drag terms for motion planning on tilted substrates., Conveyor/process engineering: computing orientation-dependent resistance terms for material transport on inclined paths., Biomechanics/prosthetics: modeling posture-angle-dependent dissipative load components during gait or assistive actuation., Aerospace/attitude systems: projecting orientation-dependent parasitic load terms in simplified vehicle dynamics.

pdg_node.informal_desc: Compute gravity-induced force component along the road grade.

declaration.name: compute_gravity_grade_force
declaration.docstring: Compute gravity-induced force component along the road grade.
declaration.conceptual_summary: signed_trigonometric_projection_of_constant_vector_field. Transforms an orientation scalar into the directed component of an otherwise constant-magnitude vector field along a chosen axis. The operation applies a trigonometric projection (typically proportional to sin(theta)) with a fixed scale factor from model constants, yielding a signed scalar that indicates direction and intensity along that axis. This is a coordinate-resolution step from angular representation to 1D effective loading.. Applications: Resolving a uniform field load onto a tilted microfluidic channel axis to estimate net transport-driving component., Computing the component of a constant inertial/body-force vector along a robotic link’s motion axis from joint orientation., Projecting environmental acceleration onto an instrument-sensitive axis in inertial sensing and calibration pipelines., Estimating downslope tangential stress component from terrain inclination in geomechanical stability screening.

declaration.docstring: Compute state derivatives for the system dynamics.

declaration.type_signature: (x: state vector, u: input/control vector, _t: float) -> state derivative vector
declaration.docstring: Compute state derivatives for the system dynamics.

pdg_node.statement: (x: state vector, _u: input/control vector, _t: float) -> matrix
pdg_node.informal_desc: Compute Jacobian of system dynamics with respect to state.

declaration.name: linearize_dynamics
declaration.type_signature: (x: state vector, _u: input/control vector, _t: float) -> matrix
declaration.docstring: Compute Jacobian of system dynamics with respect to state.
declaration.conceptual_summary: Local Tangent Operator Computation. Evaluates a vector-valued transition function at a specific point and computes its partial derivatives with respect to the primary state coordinates. The result is a first-order linear operator that approximates how infinitesimal perturbations in the input state propagate to perturbations in the next state. This is a local sensitivity map on the tangent space of the original nonlinear transform.. Applications: Stability and sensitivity analysis of biochemical reaction-network models near an operating point, Linearized error propagation in aerospace guidance and navigation estimators, Small-signal analysis of electric power network dynamics around nominal conditions, Local behavior analysis of ecological interaction models near equilibrium states

declaration.name: linearize_dynamics

pdg_node.statement: (x: state vector, x_dot_desired: state derivative vector, _t: float) -> input/control vector
pdg_node.informal_desc: Compute control input required to match desired state derivative.

declaration.name: solve_control_for_target_derivative
declaration.type_signature: (x: state vector, x_dot_desired: state derivative vector, _t: float) -> input/control vector
declaration.docstring: Compute control input required to match desired state derivative.
declaration.conceptual_summary: instantaneous inverse state-rate actuation solver. This atom maps a current state vector and a target first-order state-rate vector to an actuation vector by inverting the model's differential relation at a single instant. Formally, it solves an algebraic inverse problem of the form find u such that f(x, u, t) matches the requested state derivative. The result is an immediate command-level solution, not a multi-step optimization over a horizon.. Applications: Aerospace guidance: compute thruster or control-surface commands to realize a desired attitude-rate vector at the current state., Bioreactor automation: derive pump/heater actuation to enforce a target rate of change in concentration-temperature state variables., Smart-grid power electronics: solve inverter command vectors to produce desired derivatives of voltage-frequency state coordinates., Musculoskeletal assistive systems: map desired joint-state velocity/acceleration profiles to actuator command vectors in wearable devices.

pdg_node.informal_desc: Load model parameters from file and construct model-ready data.

declaration.name: deserialize_model_spec
declaration.docstring: Load model parameters from file and construct model-ready data.
declaration.conceptual_summary: Serialized Specification Materialization. Reads a serialized specification from persistent storage and parses it into a typed in-memory parameter structure. The operation validates and normalizes fields against expected schema constraints, then instantiates a fully configured executable object with internal state derived from those parameters. The result is a runtime-ready computational artifact equivalent to the persisted specification.. Applications: Hydrology: load watershed simulation constants and boundary conditions into a solver instance, Robotics: reconstruct control-policy parameters and actuator constraints for a deployment runtime, Computational chemistry: instantiate molecular simulation settings and force-field coefficients from serialized config, Telecommunications: initialize signal-processing pipeline stages from a persisted processing graph specification
